root_cause_class: unbounded execution stage / missing checkpoint breaker
invariant: Every governed execution stage must have explicit timeout, scope, checkpoint, and partial-receipt behavior. If a stage appears to hang or loop, stop, do not claim success, write a partial receipt if possible, and resume from the last verified artifact.
required_future_behavior: Use subprocess timeouts for every external command.
Bound recursive scans by path, file type, file size, count, or explicit manifest.
Write a partial receipt on interruption, timeout, or suspected runaway execution.
Fail closed instead of claiming success when a stage is interrupted or unverified.
Resume from artifacts and receipts, not conversational memory.
Prefer smaller recovery stages after timeout, tool instability, or repeated execution failure.
